sections.paragraph:
βούλησις, εως, ἡ (Eur., Thu.+; Epict., ins; PTebt 43, 35; PHamb 73, 10; POxy 2267, 11; TestSol 1:3 D; Jos., C. Ap. 1, 45) the act of willing, will, of God’s will (Plato, Tim. 41b; Ephorus [IV b.c.]: 70 Fgm. 31b Jac.; Diod S 17, 66, 7; Phlegon: 257 Fgm. 36, 1, 1; 36, 1, 4; 36, 1, 7 Jac.; Parthenius 4, 5; 15, 4 β. θεῶν; OGI 458, 15; 669, 17; PAmh 144, 11; POxy 130, 11; expressed in a dream, UPZ 81 IV, 19 [II b.c.]; EpArist 234; Philo, Rer. Div. Her. 246; Jos., Ant. 2, 232; Just., D. 5, 4; 63, 1; Eupolemus: 723 Fgm. 2b, 2 [in Eus., PE 9, 30, 2]) ἡ ἔνδοξος β. αὐτοῦ 1 Cl 9:1; ἡ ἄμωμος β. αὐτοῦ 35:5; ἡ ὑπέρτατος αὐτοῦ β. 40:3; παρὰ τὸ καθῆκον τῆς βουλήσεως αὐτοῦ contrary to what conforms to his will 41:3.—The history of tradition of the Lat. text (voluntas) of 42:4 is uncertain.—DELG s.v. βούλομαι. TW.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

PHamb PHamb = Griechische Papyrusurkunden der Hamburger Staats- und Universitätsbibliothek—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Phlegon Phlegon , II a.d.—List 5

Parthenius Parthenius , I b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Lat. Lat. = Latin

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 182
